feed.title: Scholar Alerts — Clean Feed
feed.subtitle: Google Scholar alerts reformatted as individual article entries

entry.title: Spiking neuromorphic circuits for solving finite element problems
entry.content:
Authors: BH Theilman, JB Aimone - US Patent App. 18/812,419, 2026

Abstract: A spiking neuromorphic circuit that instantiates a finite element methods (FEM) mesh is provided. The circuit comprises a number of groups of spiking neurons, wherein each group of spiking neurons represents a mesh node in the FEM mesh, wherein ...

Alert: James B Aimone (author alert)

entry.title: Biomining of Lunar-Relevant Materials under Simulated Lunar Gravity on the International Space Station
entry.content:
Authors: A Acciardo, R Santomartino, C Cockell, C Magnabosco...

Journal: 2026

Abstract: With near-future manned space exploration expanding beyond low Earth orbit out toward the Moon and beyond, there is a critical need to understand how a sustained human lunar presence can be supported through in-situ resource utilization (ISRU) ...

Alert: Charles Cockell (author alert)

entry.title: Early to Middle Miocene terrestrial paleoenvironmental reconstructions of the Dinarides Lake System (Croatia, Bosnia and Herzegovina)
entry.content:
Authors: M Moreau, M Löberbauer, G Sanchez-Ortiz, O Mandic...

Journal: 2026

Abstract: The Dinarides Lake System (DLS) constituted a set of intramontane freshwater lakes, which expanded during the Early to Middle Miocene in southeastern Europe. Sedimentation between ca. 18-12.5 Ma was contemporaneous with two well ...

Alert: Gerald Auer (author alert)

entry.title: Calcite vein microstructures along the South Atlantic Transect: Implications for intraplate stress variations
entry.content:
Authors: W Kurz, L Eckstein, G Auer, T Kunkelova, T Müller...

Journal: 2026

Abstract: The South Atlantic Transect (SAT) is a multidisciplinary scientific ocean drilling experiment designed to investigate the evolution of the ocean crust and overlying sediments across the western flank of the Mid-Atlantic Ridge (Coggon et al., 2024) ...

Alert: Gerald Auer (author alert)

Abstract: TYPE Original Research PUBLISHED 02 August 2022 DOI 10.3389/fsufs.2022.937200 Mining alleles for tar spot OPEN ACCESS complex resistance from EDITED BY Stefania Masci, CIMMYT's maize Germplasm University of Tuscia, Italy REVIEWED ...

Alert: Kelly Swarts (author alert)

entry.title: Climate-Driven Slope Instability: Landslide Hazard at Danube riverside slopes (Hungary)
entry.content:
Authors: Á Török, A Kis, B Turák, S Rózsa

Journal: 2026

Abstract: Slope movements are among the most widespread and damaging natural hazards in Hungary and worldwide. In recent decades, the occurrence and impact of landslides and related mass movements have markedly increased, a trend commonly linked to ...

Alert: Török, Ákos (author alert)
